description_long: This object is a stylized model of an airplane with a sharply pointed nose and a streamlined fuselage, mounted on a circular base. The wings are swept back and appear to be integrated into the main body, giving the aircraft a delta-wing appearance. The tail section is raised and features a single vertical stabilizer, with no visible horizontal stabilizers extending from the tail. The surface is uniformly colored and lacks detailed markings, windows, or visible engines, suggesting a simplified or abstract representation. The airplane is elevated above the base by a single support, making it appear as if it is in flight. The overall form is sleek and angular, with minimal surface detail.
description: A sleek, delta-wing airplane model mounted on a circular base, featuring a pointed nose and a single vertical tail fin.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from a front-left angle, highlighting the pointed nose, swept wings, and the circular base supporting the model.
description_view_1: A side view showing the elongated fuselage, the sharp nose, and the way the airplane is elevated above the base by a single support.
description_view_2: A rear view emphasizing the single vertical tail fin and the broad, flat wings extending from the fuselage.
description_view_3: A rear-right angle that showcases the delta-wing configuration and the raised tail, with the support connecting to the base clearly visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Delta-wing configuration with swept-back wings integrated into the fuselage; no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines present on the model.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer; no visible horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_cockpit_placement: No visible cockpit or canopy features present on the fuselage.
